meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master SaaS development with our advanced starter kit. Overcome lack of SaaS knowledge using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship projects at record speed.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of SaaS knowledge, leverage a pre-architected SaaS starter kit that integrates authentication, billing, and database schemas out-of-the-box. By using Antigravity for rapid boilerplate generation and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you can bypass months of learning and ship production-ready SaaS applications in days.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the weight of a client’s vision pressing down, and the crushing realization that 'building a SaaS' involves a labyrinth of auth, billing, and database migrations you haven't mastered yet. The industry moves too fast for traditional learning curves; while you’re reading documentation, your competitors are shipping features.

But the game has shifted. By treating your development environment as a product factory rather than a coding station, you can bridge the knowledge gap instantly. We’re moving beyond writing boilerplate—we’re deploying architectures that solve the heavy lifting for you, allowing you to focus on the unique value proposition that wins your clients.

steps.title: Initialize SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish a production-grade foundation with pre-configured auth and database schemas to eliminate technical debt from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS starter kit with Next.js, Supabase auth, Stripe subscription integration, and Tailwind CSS. Include a modular folder structure for API routes and database migrations.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Ensure your data layer is scalable and type-safe without manually writing complex SQL boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for Organizations, Users, and Subscriptions. Add RLS policies for data isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'npx prisma migrate dev --name init_saas_schema'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Flow
steps.description: Integrate payments securely without getting lost in Stripe's extensive documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checkout flow using Stripe Webhooks to handle subscription events, including 'customer.subscription.created' and 'invoice.payment_succeeded'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && npx stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe'

steps.title: Configure Multi-Tenant Auth
steps.description: Force isolation between your client's customers using secure, session-based authentication providers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement middleware to restrict access to authenticated users and ensure they can only view data scoped to their organization_id.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'src/middleware.ts' --instruction 'enforce tenant isolation via organization_id on all API routes'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Pipeline
steps.description: Automate your CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is tested and deployed with zero manual configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx vercel build && npx vercel deploy --prod'

pro_tips: Always define your data schema in a separate 'schema.prisma' file before touching frontend code to ensure a single source of truth.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints immediately after generation; it catches edge cases that manual QA misses.
Never hardcode Stripe IDs; use a .env.local pattern managed by your starter kit for environment-specific keys.
Leverage Antigravity's 'context-aware' mode to keep your codebase aligned with current best practices for Next.js 14+ server actions.
When a feature gets complex, use Claude Code to 'refactor' your existing functions into modular services to improve maintainability for future client handoffs.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, focus on implementing a feedback loop using user analytics. Iterate based on actual customer usage rather than feature bloat.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the toolkit to outpace the competition and deliver high-margin SaaS projects for your clients without the headache of manual setup. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
